project.creator: SonNT
environment: 1. Frontend: React subscription pages at /client/subscription, /broker/subscription, and /freelancer/subscription
2. Backend: NestJS subscriptions module and quota service under /subscriptions
3. Database: PostgreSQL subscription_plans, user_subscriptions, and quota_usage_logs tables with seeded premium plans
project.notes: Feature-level integration workbook for FN-37 Subscription Management covering UC-39 View Subscription, UC-40 Register New Subscription, and UC-41 Cancel Subscription.

changeLog.description: Initial integration test workbook for plan listing, subscription status, premium activation, and soft-cancel flows.

features.name: Subscription Management
features.testRequirement: Validate role-scoped premium plan listing, current subscription status retrieval, premium subscription activation for supported billing cycles, duplicate and role-mismatch guards, and soft-cancel behavior that preserves perks until period end.

functions.name: Subscription Management
functions.description: Authenticated users review their current plan, browse role-specific premium plans, subscribe with a billing cycle, and cancel via soft-cancel semantics.
functions.preCondition: JWT authentication succeeds and subscription plans are seeded for the caller role.

testCases.description: Return only active subscription plans that match the authenticated user's role.
testCases.procedure:
1. Authenticate as a CLIENT role user.
2. Call GET /subscriptions/plans.
3. Seed active CLIENT_PREMIUM plus active plans for other roles.
4. Inspect the returned plan collection.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. SubscriptionsController.getPlans reads req.user.role.
2. SubscriptionsService.getPlansForRole filters by role and isActive = true.
3. The response contains only CLIENT plans sorted by displayOrder.
4. Each item includes monthly, quarterly, yearly pricing and perks.
testCases.dependencies: Seeded subscription_plans rows exist for CLIENT, BROKER, and FREELANCER roles.

testCases.description: Show free-tier subscription status, role-specific perks, and usage when no active premium subscription exists.
testCases.procedure: 1. Authenticate as a broker without any ACTIVE or CANCELLED subscription whose currentPeriodEnd is in the future.\n2. Open the SubscriptionPage or call GET /subscriptions/me.\n3. Inspect the response and rendered Current Plan card.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. getActiveSubscription returns null.
2. getMySubscription sets isPremium = false and subscription = null.
3. FREE_TIER_PERKS for BROKER are returned in perks.
4. QuotaService usage summary is included in the response for display.
testCases.dependencies: The authenticated broker exists and has no current premium record.
testCases.note: Covers the free-tier branch used by SubscriptionPage when rendering Free Plan.

testCases.description: Show premium subscription details and keep premium access when the subscription is CANCELLED but still within the billing period.
testCases.procedure: 1. Seed a CANCELLED user_subscriptions row for a freelancer where currentPeriodEnd is still in the future and cancelAtPeriodEnd = true.\n2. Authenticate as that freelancer.\n3. Call GET /subscriptions/me and inspect the UI state.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. getActiveSubscription returns the CANCELLED record because currentPeriodEnd >= now.
2. isPremium is true.
3. The response includes plan details, amountPaid, currentPeriodEnd, and cancelAtPeriodEnd = true.
4. SubscriptionPage shows the cancellation-pending alert instead of the free-tier upgrade call to action.
testCases.dependencies: A cancelled-but-active subscription exists for the authenticated freelancer and links to a seeded plan.
testCases.note: Covers soft-cancel visibility after cancellation but before expiration.

testCases.description: Create a monthly premium subscription successfully for a role-matching plan.
testCases.procedure:
1. Authenticate as a client without an active subscription.
2. Open the subscription page and keep the Monthly billing cycle selected.
3. Click Subscribe Now for the Premium Client plan or call POST /subscriptions/subscribe with planId and billingCycle = MONTHLY.
4. Reload GET /subscriptions/me.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. subscribe validates that no active subscription already exists.
2. The selected plan is found and its role matches CLIENT.
3. A new user_subscriptions row is saved with status ACTIVE, cancelAtPeriodEnd = false, and amountPaid = priceMonthly.
4. currentPeriodEnd is about 30 days after currentPeriodStart.
5. The follow-up status call shows isPremium = true.
testCases.dependencies: A seeded active CLIENT_PREMIUM plan exists and the caller has no current premium record.
testCases.note: Positive subscribe flow for monthly billing.

testCases.description: Create a quarterly premium subscription with the discounted quarterly price and 90-day period.
testCases.procedure:
1. Authenticate as a broker without an active subscription.
2. Select the Quarterly billing cycle on the SubscriptionPage.
3. Submit POST /subscriptions/subscribe using the broker plan and billingCycle = QUARTERLY.
4. Inspect the saved subscription and response payload.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. subscribe uses CYCLE_DURATION_DAYS[QUARTERLY] = 90 to compute currentPeriodEnd.
2. amountPaid equals the plan's priceQuarterly.
3. billingCycle is stored as QUARTERLY.
4. The success response includes the new id, ACTIVE status, dates, and amountPaid.
testCases.dependencies: An active BROKER_PREMIUM plan exists and the caller has no active subscription.
testCases.note: Covers non-default pricing and duration selection.

testCases.description: Reject a subscribe request when the user already has an active or cancelled-but-active premium subscription.
testCases.procedure: 1. Seed an ACTIVE or future-dated CANCELLED subscription for a client.\n2. Authenticate as that client.\n3. Submit POST /subscriptions/subscribe with any valid client plan id.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. getActiveSubscription returns the existing current subscription.
2. subscribe throws ConflictException.
3. The response message tells the user to cancel the current plan before subscribing to a new one.
4. No extra user_subscriptions row is created.
testCases.dependencies: The caller already has a current subscription record whose period has not ended.

testCases.description: Reject a subscribe request when the selected plan belongs to a different user role.
testCases.procedure: 1. Authenticate as a FREELANCER.\n2. Submit POST /subscriptions/subscribe using a CLIENT_PREMIUM or BROKER_PREMIUM plan id.\n3. Inspect the error payload and subscription table.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The plan is loaded successfully but plan.role does not match user.role.\n2. subscribe throws BadRequestException indicating the target plan role and the caller role.\n3. No subscription row is saved.
testCases.dependencies: The selected plan exists and is active but targets a different role from the caller.

testCases.description: Cancel an active premium subscription through the soft-cancel flow while preserving perks until period end.
testCases.procedure:
1. Seed an ACTIVE Premium Freelancer subscription with a future currentPeriodEnd.
2. Authenticate as that freelancer.
3. Open the cancel modal, optionally enter a reason, and submit POST /subscriptions/cancel.
4. Reload GET /subscriptions/me.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. cancel locates the ACTIVE subscription for the user.
2. cancelAtPeriodEnd becomes true, cancelledAt is set, cancelReason stores the provided reason, and status becomes CANCELLED.
3. The success message states that premium perks remain active until currentPeriodEnd.
4. A follow-up status request still reports isPremium = true while the billing period remains active.
testCases.dependencies: An ACTIVE subscription exists for the authenticated user and its currentPeriodEnd is in the future.
testCases.note: Positive soft-cancel flow including UI modal and API response.

testCases.description: Reject cancellation when the active subscription is already scheduled for cancellation.
testCases.procedure: 1. Seed a subscription row with status ACTIVE and cancelAtPeriodEnd = true for a broker.\n2. Authenticate as that broker.\n3. Submit POST /subscriptions/cancel again.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. cancel finds the ACTIVE record.
2. The service detects cancelAtPeriodEnd = true.
3. ConflictException is returned with a message that cancellation is already scheduled.
4. Existing cancellation metadata remains unchanged.
testCases.dependencies: An ACTIVE subscription exists with cancelAtPeriodEnd already set to true.

testCases.description: Return not found when a free-tier user attempts to cancel without any active premium subscription.
testCases.procedure: 1. Authenticate as a client with no ACTIVE subscription row.\n2. Submit POST /subscriptions/cancel.\n3. Inspect the response payload.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. subscriptionRepo.findOne returns null for status ACTIVE.\n2. cancel throws NotFoundException stating that no active subscription exists and the user is on the free plan.\n3. No subscription data is modified.
testCases.dependencies: The caller does not have any ACTIVE user_subscriptions record.
